core_mechanism: recasting a stationary boundary-value (spatial) problem as an equivalent initial-value evolution problem generated by an effective non-Hermitian (non-self-adjoint) linear operator, so that one spatial coordinate plays the role of 'time' and the evolution generated by this operator yields the exact solution
brief_reason: The abstract states the method maps the stationary scattering problem to 'quantum dynamics generated by an effective non-Hermitian Hamiltonian operator,' i.e. it converts a spatial boundary-value problem into a time-like evolution driven by a non-Hermitian generator to obtain exact analytic amplitudes.
